response:
[
  {
    "vc_id": 38409,
    "reference": "Gen 3:16",
    "strongs": "H6089A",
    "transliteration": "e.tsev",
    "gloss": "toil",
    "meaning": "The woman's pain in childbearing is divinely multiplied as a consequence of the fall; it is a bodily and inner anguish imposed on her in the most intimate act of creation, linking suffering to her core vocation."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38421,
    "reference": "Gen 3:16",
    "strongs": "H6093",
    "transliteration": "its.tsa.von",
    "gloss": "toil",
    "meaning": "The woman's toil is expressed as multiplied pain in childbearing, a curse-laden inner suffering tied to the bodily experience of bringing forth life after the fall."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38420,
    "reference": "Gen 3:17",
    "strongs": "H6093",
    "transliteration": "its.tsa.von",
    "gloss": "toil",
    "meaning": "Adam's toil is a painful, grinding labor imposed on him through the cursed ground all the days of his life; the suffering is ongoing and existential, touching his will and daily effort."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38422,
    "reference": "Gen 5:29",
    "strongs": "H6093",
    "transliteration": "its.tsa.von",
    "gloss": "toil",
    "meaning": "The painful toil of human hands is felt as a collective burden from the cursed ground; Noah is named in hope that he will bring relief from this inner and physical weariness."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13241,
    "reference": "Gen 6:5",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Evil here is not distress suffered but distress inflicted — every intention of the heart's thoughts is only evil continually, showing wickedness as the total orientation of the inner person."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54480,
    "reference": "Gen 21:16",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Hagar weeps aloud in desperation, unable to watch her child die; the weeping is the outward expression of her inner anguish and helplessness in the face of imminent loss."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54478,
    "reference": "Gen 23:2",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Abraham weeps for Sarah after her death; the weeping is paired with mourning and directed at his wife, expressing the inner grief of bereavement and personal loss."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29964,
    "reference": "Gen 23:2",
    "strongs": "H5594",
    "transliteration": "sa.phad",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Abraham goes in deliberately to mourn for Sarah; the mourning is an intentional, relational act of the will directed toward his deceased wife, expressing inner grief through ritual lamentation."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18600,
    "reference": "Gen 26:35",
    "strongs": "H4786",
    "transliteration": "mo.rah",
    "gloss": "bitterness",
    "meaning": "Esau's foreign wives made life itself bitter for Isaac and Rebekah; the bitterness is a pervasive inner distress inflicted on the parents by the relational and spiritual friction of these marriages."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54504,
    "reference": "Gen 27:38",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Esau weeps aloud before his father after learning the blessing is gone; the weeping is driven by desperate longing and the inner wound of irreversible loss of paternal blessing."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38161,
    "reference": "Gen 27:41",
    "strongs": "H0060",
    "transliteration": "e.vel",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "Esau privately anticipates the mourning period for his father as a future window for revenge; mourning here is a communal, time-bound grief that paradoxically frames his murderous intent."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54555,
    "reference": "Gen 29:11",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Jacob weeps aloud upon meeting Rachel; the weeping flows from strong emotional overflow — joy, relief, and tender feeling — at encountering his kin after a long journey."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54556,
    "reference": "Gen 33:4",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Both Esau and Jacob weep in their reunion embrace; the mutual weeping expresses the release of long-held fear and grief, and the restoration of a fractured brotherly bond."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65125,
    "reference": "Gen 35:3",
    "strongs": "H6869B",
    "transliteration": "tsa.rah",
    "gloss": "distress",
    "meaning": "Jacob recalls God answering him in his day of distress; the distress was a past inner crisis that drove him to cry out to God, who proved faithful and present through it."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18490,
    "reference": "Gen 37:34",
    "strongs": "H0056",
    "transliteration": "a.val",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Jacob tears his garments, puts on sackcloth, and mourns for his son many days; his mourning is prolonged, embodied, and directed entirely at the believed death of Joseph, expressing inconsolable inner grief."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18528,
    "reference": "Gen 37:35",
    "strongs": "H0057",
    "transliteration": "a.vel",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "Jacob refuses all comfort and declares he will go down to Sheol mourning; his mourning is a total inner condition he intends to carry to death, resisting any relief from his grief over Joseph."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54505,
    "reference": "Gen 37:35",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Jacob's father weeps for him — the verse closes with paternal grief that is unrelieved and continuing, the inner sorrow of a father who believes his son is dead."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13244,
    "reference": "Gen 39:9",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Joseph frames the proposed act as 'great wickedness' and 'sin against God'; the evil here is a moral quality perceived by the conscience, restraining him from betraying his master and God."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65056,
    "reference": "Gen 41:51",
    "strongs": "H5999",
    "transliteration": "a.mal",
    "gloss": "trouble",
    "meaning": "Joseph names his son Manasseh because God made him forget all his hardship; the trouble was a deep inner burden of suffering and estrangement that God's blessing caused him to release from memory."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65126,
    "reference": "Gen 42:21",
    "strongs": "H6869B",
    "transliteration": "tsa.rah",
    "gloss": "distress",
    "meaning": "The brothers recall seeing Joseph's soul in distress as he begged them; the distress was an acute inner anguish of soul, witnessed and ignored, now returning upon the brothers as guilt and consequence."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54557,
    "reference": "Gen 42:24",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph turns away from his brothers and weeps privately; the weeping reveals inner emotional intensity — recognition, longing, and grief — that he cannot yet express openly to them."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 850,
    "reference": "Gen 42:38",
    "strongs": "H3015",
    "transliteration": "ya.gon",
    "gloss": "sorrow",
    "meaning": "Jacob fears that losing Benjamin would drive him down to Sheol in sorrow; the sorrow is an overwhelming inner grief that he anticipates would consume him unto death, compounded by accumulated loss."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54558,
    "reference": "Gen 43:30",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph's compassion for his brother Benjamin surges and he must find a private place to weep; the weeping is the physical outlet of an inner emotional flood he cannot suppress in public."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13243,
    "reference": "Gen 44:4",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Joseph accuses the brothers of repaying evil for good; the evil here is an ingratitude-driven moral wrongdoing — a relational betrayal of beneficence — framed as an inner disposition acted out."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13242,
    "reference": "Gen 44:29",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Jacob fears harm descending upon Benjamin would drive him to Sheol 'in evil'; the evil is the distressing catastrophe of compounding loss that would overwhelm and destroy him inwardly."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 851,
    "reference": "Gen 44:31",
    "strongs": "H3015",
    "transliteration": "ya.gon",
    "gloss": "sorrow",
    "meaning": "The brothers warn that Jacob would die of sorrow if Benjamin did not return; sorrow is so total an inner experience for the already-grieving father that it is treated as literally lethal."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54581,
    "reference": "Gen 45:2",
    "strongs": "H1065",
    "transliteration": "be.khi",
    "gloss": "weeping",
    "meaning": "Joseph's weeping at his self-revelation is so loud it is heard throughout Pharaoh's household; the weeping is an uncontainable outpouring of pent-up emotion — grief, relief, and joy — breaking through publicly."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54553,
    "reference": "Gen 45:14",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph and Benjamin weep on each other's necks at their reunion; the mutual weeping expresses the deep inner bond and long-suppressed grief of separated brothers finally restored to one another."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54481,
    "reference": "Gen 45:15",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph kisses all his brothers and weeps upon them before they can speak freely; the weeping is an act of emotional release and reconciliation, clearing the relational breach through tears before words."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54560,
    "reference": "Gen 46:29",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph falls on his father Israel's neck and weeps a long while; the prolonged weeping reflects the depth of his inner longing and grief at years of separation, now resolved in reunion."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54482,
    "reference": "Gen 50:1",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph falls on his dead father's face and weeps over him; the weeping is the immediate inner response of filial grief at the moment of bereavement, paired with a final kiss of love."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54483,
    "reference": "Gen 50:3",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "The Egyptians weep for Jacob seventy days, a mourning period matching the embalming time; the weeping is a communal, sustained expression of grief and honor for a figure of great significance."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54608,
    "reference": "Gen 50:4",
    "strongs": "H1068",
    "transliteration": "be.khit",
    "gloss": "weeping",
    "meaning": "The days of weeping for Jacob are described as a defined, concluded period; this frames grief as a bounded communal experience with an end point, after which practical action resumes."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38162,
    "reference": "Gen 50:10",
    "strongs": "H0060",
    "transliteration": "e.vel",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "Joseph makes a seven-day mourning for his father at the threshing floor of Atad; the mourning is a formal, communal act of extended grief honoring the dead, giving public shape to inner sorrow."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29951,
    "reference": "Gen 50:10",
    "strongs": "H4553",
    "transliteration": "mis.ped",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "The lamentation at Atad is described as very great and grievous; the mourning is an intense, publicly expressed communal grief, proportional to the greatness of the loss and the one being mourned."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29967,
    "reference": "Gen 50:10",
    "strongs": "H5594",
    "transliteration": "sa.phad",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "The company laments together at Atad with great and grievous lamentation; mourning here is a collective, ceremonial act of grief that externalizes the inner sorrow of a large company of mourners."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38163,
    "reference": "Gen 50:11",
    "strongs": "H0060",
    "transliteration": "e.vel",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "The Canaanites observe and name the mourning as 'grievous mourning by the Egyptians'; the mourning is visibly intense enough to define a place, showing grief as a social phenomenon witnessed from outside."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13260,
    "reference": "Gen 50:15",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "The brothers fear Joseph will repay them for the evil they did him; the evil is their past moral wrongdoing against him, now stirring fear in their consciences about deserved retribution."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54559,
    "reference": "Gen 50:17",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Joseph weeps when his brothers appeal for forgiveness; the weeping shows that the appeal moves him deeply — his inner response is grief and compassion, not anger or hardness."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13269,
    "reference": "Gen 50:17",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "The brothers confess they did evil to Joseph; the evil is a moral wrong they now openly name with guilt, appealing for forgiveness — their conscience recognizes the harm they willfully caused."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13245,
    "reference": "Gen 50:20",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Joseph acknowledges his brothers intended evil against him but declares God intended it for good; the evil was a deliberate, malicious inner intention, reframed by divine providence without being minimized."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54569,
    "reference": "Exo 2:6",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "The infant Moses is crying when Pharaoh's daughter opens the basket; the weeping is a baby's cry of distress that evokes her pity, making his vulnerability and need visible and heard."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18684,
    "reference": "Exo 2:23",
    "strongs": "H0584",
    "transliteration": "a.nach",
    "gloss": "to sigh",
    "meaning": "Israel groans and sighs under slavery and their cry rises to God; the sighing is the involuntary inner expression of a people whose suffering is so heavy it escapes them as a groan seeking divine attention."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18724,
    "reference": "Exo 2:24",
    "strongs": "H5009",
    "transliteration": "ne.a.qah",
    "gloss": "groan",
    "meaning": "God hears Israel's groaning under slavery and remembers his covenant; the groan is the inner cry of oppressed suffering that rises to God and triggers covenantal faithfulness and action."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18586,
    "reference": "Exo 3:7",
    "strongs": "H4341",
    "transliteration": "makh.ov",
    "gloss": "pain",
    "meaning": "God declares he knows Israel's sufferings under Egypt; the pain is the inward experience of affliction that God perceives and acknowledges, establishing his empathy as the basis for his redemptive intervention."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18725,
    "reference": "Exo 6:5",
    "strongs": "H5009",
    "transliteration": "ne.a.qah",
    "gloss": "groan",
    "meaning": "God hears the groaning of Israel enslaved by Egypt and responds by remembering his covenant; the groaning is the inner voice of communal suffering that reaches and moves God to act."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13246,
    "reference": "Exo 10:10",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Pharaoh accuses Moses and the Israelites of having evil purpose in mind; the evil is a malicious inner intention he attributes to them, using it to justify his refusal to release them."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13247,
    "reference": "Exo 23:2",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Israel is commanded not to follow the crowd in doing evil or perverting justice; the evil is a social pressure threatening the individual's inner moral integrity and conscience in legal and communal settings."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18491,
    "reference": "Exo 33:4",
    "strongs": "H0056",
    "transliteration": "a.val",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "The people mourn when they hear God's disastrous word and remove their ornaments; the mourning is a collective inner and outward response of grief and contrition to the threat of divine withdrawal."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54508,
    "reference": "Lev 10:6",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "The whole house of Israel is told to bewail the burning of Nadab and Abihu; weeping here is a communal grief-duty assigned to the congregation while the priests must restrain personal mourning under threat of death."
  }
]

[
  {
    "vc_id": 1186,
    "reference": "Hab 1:3",
    "strongs": "H5999",
    "transliteration": "a.mal",
    "gloss": "trouble",
    "meaning": "The prophet's inner being is burdened by being forced to witness iniquity, wrong, destruction, and violence—trouble here is the anguish of a watching conscience unable to reconcile what it sees with God's apparent inaction."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1201,
    "reference": "Hab 1:3",
    "strongs": "H5999",
    "transliteration": "a.mal",
    "gloss": "trouble",
    "meaning": "The prophet's inner being is burdened by being forced to witness iniquity, wrong, destruction, and violence—trouble here is the anguish of a watching conscience unable to reconcile what it sees with God's apparent inaction."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1202,
    "reference": "Hab 1:13",
    "strongs": "H5999",
    "transliteration": "a.mal",
    "gloss": "trouble",
    "meaning": "Trouble here is the morally repugnant wrongdoing that God, being too pure to look at evil, nonetheless appears to tolerate—the inner distress arises from the perceived contradiction between divine holiness and divine silence."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1945,
    "reference": "Zep 1:15",
    "strongs": "H4691",
    "transliteration": "me.tsu.qah",
    "gloss": "distress",
    "meaning": "Distress is one element of the overwhelming inner and external anguish of God's day of wrath, paired with anguish and surrounded by ruin and darkness, conveying total crushing pressure on those who experience it."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65175,
    "reference": "Zep 1:15",
    "strongs": "H6869B",
    "transliteration": "tsa.rah",
    "gloss": "distress",
    "meaning": "Distress is the first-named inner quality of the coming day of wrath, paired immediately with anguish, marking it as a day of intense emotional and existential constriction for those under divine judgment."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54538,
    "reference": "Zec 7:3",
    "strongs": "H1058",
    "transliteration": "ba.khah",
    "gloss": "to weep",
    "meaning": "Weeping here is a ritualized expression of grief performed annually in the fifth month for many years, raising the question of whether such outward mourning is directed genuinely toward God or is merely habitual practice."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29991,
    "reference": "Zec 7:5",
    "strongs": "H5594",
    "transliteration": "sa.phad",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Mourning here is the communal ritual of fasting and lamenting observed over seventy years, which God questions as to its true inward orientation—whether the grief was genuinely directed toward him or performed for the people's own purposes."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13259,
    "reference": "Zec 7:10",
    "strongs": "H7451I",
    "transliteration": "ra.ah",
    "gloss": "distress: evil",
    "meaning": "Evil here is conceived inwardly—it is something devised in the heart against another person—showing that this harmful distress originates in the will and intention before it is acted out."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29961,
    "reference": "Zec 12:10",
    "strongs": "H4553",
    "transliteration": "mis.ped",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "Mourning here is the intense personal grief that will arise when Jerusalem looks on the one they pierced, compared to the deepest possible human sorrow—the loss of an only child or firstborn—signaling a profound, heart-level lamentation."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29966,
    "reference": "Zec 12:10",
    "strongs": "H5594",
    "transliteration": "sa.phad",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Mourning here is the inner response that will be poured out by divine grace when Jerusalem sees the one they pierced—a deep, grief-stricken lamentation likened to the bitterest personal loss of an only child."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29962,
    "reference": "Zec 12:11",
    "strongs": "H4553",
    "transliteration": "mis.ped",
    "gloss": "mourning",
    "meaning": "Mourning in Jerusalem on that day will be of extraordinary magnitude, compared to the famously intense corporate grief over Hadad-rimmon at Megiddo, indicating a communal inner sorrow of historic proportions."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 29992,
    "reference": "Zec 12:12",
    "strongs": "H5594",
    "transliteration": "sa.phad",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Mourning is so personal and deep that even within families it separates individuals—each clan and each gender grieves apart, indicating an inward sorrow too profound to share even in community."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18695,
    "reference": "Mal 2:13",
    "strongs": "H0603",
    "transliteration": "a.na.qah",
    "gloss": "groaning",
    "meaning": "Groaning here accompanies weeping and tears brought to the altar, expressing deep emotional distress over God's rejection of the people's offerings—but the grief is outward performance rather than evidence of genuine inner repentance."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54606,
    "reference": "Mal 2:13",
    "strongs": "H1065",
    "transliteration": "be.khi",
    "gloss": "weeping",
    "meaning": "Weeping here is part of a display of grief at the altar because God no longer accepts the people's offerings, but the verse implies the weeping is a frustrated response to unanswered ritual rather than true inner contrition."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65002,
    "reference": "Mal 2:13",
    "strongs": "H1832",
    "transliteration": "dim.ah",
    "gloss": "tears",
    "meaning": "Tears physically cover the altar alongside weeping and groaning, expressing anguish over God's rejection of offerings—the outward emotional display highlights the distress of unaccepted worship rather than genuine inner penitence."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54470,
    "reference": "Mat 2:18",
    "strongs": "G2805",
    "transliteration": "klauthmos",
    "gloss": "weeping",
    "meaning": "Weeping here is the anguished, unconsolable lament of Rachel (representing Israelite mothers) over children killed by Herod—a grief so total that comfort is refused, expressing absolute inner desolation at irreversible loss."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18469,
    "reference": "Mat 5:4",
    "strongs": "G3996",
    "transliteration": "pen.the.o",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Mourning here is the inner condition of those who grieve—whether over sin, loss, or the brokenness of the world—and Jesus declares it blessed because it will be met with divine comfort, linking inner sorrow to future consolation."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 54469,
    "reference": "Mat 8:12",
    "strongs": "G2805",
    "transliteration": "klauthmos",
    "gloss": "weeping",
    "meaning": "Weeping here describes the anguished inner state of those cast into outer darkness at eschatological judgment—paired with gnashing of teeth, it conveys the full emotional and physical expression of condemned despair."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 2226,
    "reference": "Mat 8:29",
    "strongs": "G0928G",
    "transliteration": "basanizō",
    "gloss": "to torture: torture",
    "meaning": "The demons dread being tormented by Jesus before the appointed time, indicating that tormenting here refers to a severe, feared punishment they know awaits them, experienced as deeply threatening to their existence."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 18471,
    "reference": "Mat 9:15",
    "strongs": "G3996",
    "transliteration": "pen.the.o",
    "gloss": "to mourn",
    "meaning": "Mourning here is the grief appropriate to absence and loss—wedding guests cannot mourn while the bridegroom is present, but will mourn when he is taken away, tying inner sorrow directly to the experience of bereavement."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 12402,
    "reference": "Mat 9:36",
    "strongs": "G4660",
    "transliteration": "skullō",
    "gloss": "to trouble",
